stem: Using `.dot` (the operator product A·B, which tracks the ± i phase), which of the following single-qubit Pauli identities are TRUE? Select all that apply.
code:
from qiskit.quantum_info import Pauli
# e.g. Pauli('X').dot(Pauli('Y')).to_label()

explanation.correct: The cyclic products carry a +i phase: X·Y = iZ, Y·Z = iX, Z·X = iY. Reversing the order flips the sign of the phase.

distractors.D: X·Z = −iY, not +iY. This is an anti-cyclic (reversed) product, so the phase is −i.
distractors.E: Y·X = −iZ, not +iZ. It is the reverse of X·Y, so the phase flips sign.
